questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: "Water ___ at 100 degrees."
questions.explanation: A scientific fact or general truth always takes the present simple: water boils at 100 degrees. "is boiling" would mean it is happening at this moment, not always; "boil" forgets the -s for the singular subject "water"; "are boiling" wrongly treats "water" as plural.

questions.prompt: Complete: "I can't talk now, I ___ dinner."
questions.explanation: "I can't talk now" tells us the action is in progress at this moment, so we use the present continuous: I am cooking dinner. "cook"/"cooks" are present simple (habits), and "cooked" is past simple.

In the present simple negative for he/she/it, the auxiliary "does" carries the -s, so the main verb stays in its base form: she doesn't drink coffee. "don't drink" uses the wrong auxiliary for "she"; "isn't drink" mixes "be" with a base verb; "not drinks" has no auxiliary at all.

questions.prompt: Complete the question: "___ they live in Tunis?"
questions.explanation: Present simple questions use "do/does" as the auxiliary. With the plural subject "they" we use "Do" + base verb: Do they live...? "Does" is only for he/she/it; "Are"/"Is" would be needed for the present continuous (Are they living...?), not the present simple.
"taste" (= have a flavour) is stative here, so we use the present simple: the cake tastes delicious.

questions.prompt: Complete: "Be quiet! The students ___ a test right now."
questions.explanation: "right now" signals an action in progress; with the plural subject students we use are + taking.
